question: Which of the following is not a correct statement in the context of the Money Bill?
option_a: A Bill (Money Bill) is considered a Money Bill if it provides for the imposition, abolition, remission, alteration or regulation of any tax

The term "sixth mass extinction/sixth extinction" is often mentioned in the news in the context of the discussion of
option_a: Widespread monoculture practices in agriculture and large-scale commercial farming with indiscriminate use of chemicals in many parts of the world that may result in the loss of good native ecosystems.
option_b: Fears of a possible collision of a meteorite with the Earth in the near future in the manner it happened 65 million years ago that caused the mass extinction of many species including those of dinosaurs.
option_c: Large scale cultivation of genetically modified crops in many parts of the world and promoting their cultivation in other parts of the world which may cause the disappearance of good native crop plants and the loss of food biodiversity.
option_d: Mankind's over-exploitation/misuse of natural resources, fragmentation/loss of natural habitats, destruction of ecosystems, pollution and global climate change.

question: When your smart phone's alarm goes off in the morning, you get up and switch it off. Your smart mirror displays the weather for the day and the water level in your overhead tank. As you make your breakfast, you take out some grocery items from your refrigerator. The smart refrigerator informs you about the expiry of some items and gives you a shopping list. When you step out of your house and lock the door, all lights, fans, geysers and A.C. machines switch off automatically. Your car warns you about the traffic ahead on your route and suggests an alternate path. If you are likely to be late for a meeting, it sends a message to your office accordingly.
